channel.title: Java on My blog
channel.description: Recent content in Java on My blog

item.title: 'Final' keyword in Java
item.description: ‘Final’ keyword represents ‘ultimate’ and ‘unchangeable’. In this post, I will talk about four usages of the ‘final’ keyword, including modifying a class, a method, local variables (basic and reference types) and a member variable.

item.title: Java Polymorphism
item.description: In this post, I will talk about the concepts and benefits of polymorphism. Then, I will point out some important properties about using class variables and methods when polymorphism happens. Moving on, object down casting and ‘instanceof’ method will be briefly introduced. Finally, I will demonstrate a case study combining the knowledge of using interface and polymorphism.

item.title: Java Interface
item.description: An interface is a reference type in Java. It is similar to class. It is a collection of abstract methods. A class implements an interface, thereby inheriting the abstract methods of the interface. Along with abstract methods, an interface may also contain constants, default methods, static methods, and nested types (TutorialsPoint 2022).
